title: Smart retrieval + infra discovery - Nimbus Retail
preview: Discovery focused on embeddings + reranking architecture for Nimbus Retail search. Customer currently uses BM25 + in-house vector store; main concerns are throughput at peak shopping events (QPS), embedding dimensionality tradeoffs, hybrid search latency, and options for hosted vs dedicated vs private deployments (V...
text: Discovery focused on embeddings + reranking architecture for Nimbus Retail search. Customer currently uses BM25 + in-house vector store; main concerns are throughput at peak shopping events (QPS), embedding dimensionality tradeoffs, hybrid search latency, and options for hosted vs dedicated vs private deployments (VPC). Agreed to run a small throughput pilot and share security docs and pricing.

Summary:
Quick orientation for BlueHarbor on what Redwood captures in admin/activity telemetry, the export paths (push to S3, streaming webhook, syslog/third-party SIEM), and how Customer-Managed Keys (CMEK)/KMS integration works. Also covered retention options, immutable/offsite archives, a few SOC2 artifact asks, and a short demo of an event export sample.

Topics:
- Event types & schema (who/what/when/where)
- Export options (S3, streaming, connectors)
- Encryption at rest/in transit + CMEK/HSM options
- Retention, legal hold, immutable archives
- RBAC/SSO tie-ins and audi...

title: Axion Labs — inference capacity & isolation playbook sync
preview: Discovery focused on Axion's reserved GPU needs for production LLM features. Covered traffic profile, isolation requirements for regulated models, warm-pool/prewarm strategies for burst traffic, fallback to hosted variants, and security/compliance pathways (VPC, KMS, SOC2). Agreed to run a 2-week pilot with a reserv...
text: Discovery focused on Axion's reserved GPU needs for production LLM features. Covered traffic profile, isolation requirements for regulated models, warm-pool/prewarm strategies for burst traffic, fallback to hosted variants, and security/compliance pathways (VPC, KMS, SOC2). Agreed to run a 2-week pilot with a reserved small node pool and distributed load profile; Redwood to share pricing, dedicated topology diagram, and security questionnaire.

title: Controls & deployment pathways scan with Thornfield Mortgages
preview: Initial discovery to map Thornfield's data residency and logging constraints across hosted, dedicated, and private deployment options. Main concerns: EU customer PII residency, audit log retention and redaction, KMS key residency/escrow, SOC2 obligations, and how Redwood's Dedicated or Private options support tenant...
text: Initial discovery to map Thornfield's data residency and logging constraints across hosted, dedicated, and private deployment options. Main concerns: EU customer PII residency, audit log retention and redaction, KMS key residency/escrow, SOC2 obligations, and how Redwood's Dedicated or Private options support tenant isolation and legal hold. Agreed to follow up with security artifact pack and propose a Dedicated pilot for benchmarking.

title: Model Playbook & Compatibility Walkthrough - Arcadia Analytics
preview: AE + SE walked Arcadia through Redwood's model catalog, how to interpret benchmark cards and the compatibility matrix; discussed routing, quantization tradeoffs and private vs dedicated deployment options. Agreed on next steps for POC scoping and a model-fit spreadsheet.
text: AE + SE walked Arcadia through Redwood's model catalog, how to interpret benchmark cards and the compatibility matrix; discussed routing, quantization tradeoffs and private vs dedicated deployment options. Agreed on next steps for POC scoping and a model-fit spreadsheet.

text:
Meeting header:
Date: 2025-07-02 09:31 UTC
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Jordan Blake (Redwood AE), Samir Patel (Redwood SE), Maya Torres (Aperture CTO), Ethan Li (Aperture ML Eng), Priya Rao (Aperture SRE)

Summary:
Weekly pilot sync to review TTFT numbers, streaming behavior, sequence length distributions, and decide kernel selection for the next pilot iteration. Quick review of current infra (H100 reserved test nodes + one A100 fallback), discuss KV cache behavior and quantization trade-offs. Action items and next steps assigned.

Topics:
- TTFT baseline and observed p50/p95 regressions
- Streaming vs non-streaming for short-turn tasks
- Sequence length profiles and kernel selection (flash attention, flash4, cuBLAS fallbacks)
- KV cache behavior and prefix caching opportunities
- Instrumentation and traces to collect

preview: Internal SE/AE sparring session to practice contrarian objections and tighten demo messaging around latency/cost tradeoffs and private deployments. Focus on short demo scenes, phrasing for enterprise buyers (security, VPC, SOC2), and a checklist for fast-swappable model variants.

title: Hosted Gen: Interactive Showcase - Chat & Text Basics (Streams, Params, Retries)
preview: Short demo of hosted generation (chat + text), focused on streaming behavior, key parameters (temperature, max_tokens, top_p), and retry/backoff patterns. Demo included a live Playground run, quick SDK examples, and brief discussion of security (SOC2, SSO/SAML), data handling and next-step sandbox + benchmarks. Cust...
text: Short demo of hosted generation (chat + text), focused on streaming behavior, key parameters (temperature, max_tokens, top_p), and retry/backoff patterns. Demo included a live Playground run, quick SDK examples, and brief discussion of security (SOC2, SSO/SAML), data handling and next-step sandbox + benchmarks. Customer is evaluating hosted for chat feature in their mobile app; main concerns: latency, cost, and data residency.

Header:
Date: 2025-03-12
Time: 16:00 UTC
Duration: ~58 minutes
Attendees: Jordan Blake (Redwood AE), Priya Shah (SE), Evan Cole (Platform), Lisa Nguyen (Aequitas VP Product), Rahul Mehta (Head MLOps), Tom Alvarez (Security), Sana Ali (Data)

Summary:
Quick scoping call to align on POC timeline and success criteria for evaluating Redwood inference across Aequitas Health conversational and triage workloads. Primary constraints: HIPAA-adjacent security requirements, latency for clinician-facing UI, and cost per 1k tokens. Agreement to run a 4-week POC with clear milestones: kickoff, sandbox access & data onboarding, mid-point benchmark review, and exec readout.

Topics:
- POC timeline & milestones
- Access & security setup (VPC, KMS, SOC2)
- Workload sizing and benchmark profiles (seq len, concurrency, qps)
- Success criteria (latency, cost, functional parity)
- Reporting & exec readout ...

text:
Meeting header
Date: 2025-02-13
Start: 1:30 PM ET
Duration: 52 min
Attendees: Jordan Blake (Redwood), Hiro Tanaka (Redwood), Karen Voss (Orion), Miguel Santos (Orion), Elena Park (Orion)

Topics
- Offline install bundle
- What breaks when outbound is blocked
- Upgrade process in a closed enclave

Transcript (excerpt)
Jordan Blake: just to level set, when you say enclave, does the cluster have any way to reach public endpoints?
Miguel Santos: no. there is no outbound. no proxy. it's a closed network.
Hiro Tanaka: ok. that maps to what we call disconnected or air-gap installs.
Karen Voss: we try not to use air-gap as a term, but yes, it's isolated.
Jordan Blake: got it. so we'd need to provide an offline artifact bundle container images, helm charts, and checksums.
Elena Park: and we will need a list of exact image digests for security sign-off.
Miguel Santos: also anything that phones ...

MEETING HEADER
Date: 2024-10-04
Time: 11:00 CEST
Duration: 38 min

Topics (auto)
- EU data residency
- Support access and auditing

Transcript
[00:00] Jordan Blake: Today is mainly residency and what our support process looks like.
[00:35] Nina Kovacs: Our requirement is EU only. We also need to know if your support team outside EU can see anything.
[01:20] Ethan Park: Serving and storage can be in EU. For support, we have engineers globally, so the right control is RBAC + break-glass with auditing.
[03:05] Lea Martin: If someone in APAC accesses logs, is that considered data leaving the EU?
[03:22] Evelyn Hart: The audit logs remain stored in-region; the access is remote and logged. We can document the exception explicitly, limited to operational metadata only.
[06:10] Nina Kovacs: We need that written for our file. And please confirm prompts/responses aren't stored.
[06:33] Ethan Pa...
